Zima | Dania główne | Przygotowanie 20 min | Gotowanie 15 min

KOPYTKA Z GRZYBAMI

Kopytka, meaning "little hooves," are small Polish dumplings resembling their
namesake. Tossed with sautéed mushrooms and caramelized onions, this dish is
hearty comfort food at its finest. A Polish staple for family dinners.

INGREDIENTS:
- 1 1/2 lbs kopytka (or gnocchi if unavailable)
- 1 lb mixed mushrooms, sliced (cremini, oyster, shiitake)
- 2 large onions, thinly sliced
- 3 tbsp butter, divided
- 2 cloves garlic, minced
- 1/2 cup sour cream
- 1/4 cup vegetable stock
- Salt and pepper to taste
- Fresh dill for garnish
- Grated Parmesan cheese

INSTRUCTIONS:
1. Bring a large pot of salted water to boil. Cook kopytka until they float plus 2 minutes more.
2. In a large skillet, melt 1 tbsp butter over medium heat.
3. Sauté onions slowly until deep golden brown, about 20 minutes, stirring occasionally.
4. In the same skillet, melt remaining butter and add mushroom slices.
5. Cook mushrooms until golden and liquid has evaporated, about 8 minutes.
6. Add minced garlic and cook 1 minute more.
7. Stir in cooked kopytka, caramelized onions, and vegetable stock.
8. Lower heat and stir in sour cream. Season with salt and pepper.
9. Heat through gently without boiling.
10. Garnish with fresh dill and Parmesan cheese. Serve warm immediately.
